playbook.intent: Static pricing → one variable flipped for 14 days with a measured paid-conversion delta.
playbook.scope: Bi-weekly: flip one pricing variable (price, billing period, trial length, plan name) for 14 days, measure paid conversion delta vs baseline, and decide keep, rollback, or iterate.

stage.name: Hypothesis
stage.intent: One pricing variable with a directional hypothesis.

tasks.task: Pick one variable (price, billing, trial, plan name)
State expected direction of conversion change

stage.decision-gate: Advance once the hypothesis fits in one sentence.

stage.name: Toggle
stage.intent: Switch flipped in Stripe or pricing page.

tasks.task: Update marketing copy that references price

stage.decision-gate: Advance only after stranger test passes.

stage.name: Measure
stage.intent: 14 days of clean data versus baseline.

tasks.task: Pull paid conversion data at day 7 and day 14

stage.decision-gate: Advance once both checkpoints have data.

stage.name: Decide
stage.intent: Keep, rollback, or iterate with a written verdict.
stage.decision-gate: Keep only if delta exceeds threshold; otherwise rollback.

extra.value: [Full pricing rewrite, Multi-variable experiments — one knob at a time, Permanent price changes without data]
[A live pricing page with at least 30 days of baseline data, Stripe or similar checkout with toggleable pricing]
[Treating the playbook as a checklist instead of a decision tree, Skipping decision gates when intermediate progress feels good, Letting tools or platforms replace the founder's own judgement]

['Did each stage produce its named output or get explicitly skipped with a note?', 'Were decision gates respected or quietly bypassed?', 'Is the evidence trail strong enough to defend the verdict externally?']
{human: faion get-content pricing-experiment-toggle --format md, agent: faion get-content pricing-experiment-toggle --format context, json: faion get-content pricing-experiment-toggle --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: 'Initial draft from brainstorm-2026-05-17, persona P2 (indie hacker)'}]
